briefing.opening: Good morning. As the specialized spokesperson for climate change, I will begin today's climate briefing on March 9, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis, Increasing Threats to Financial and Health Security
sections.content: According to news released today, the Bank of Korea and the Financial Supervisory Service are pushing for a 'climate stress test' to assess the intensification of extreme weather events and the resulting financial and macroeconomic risks within the next five years [2, 6, 10, 11, 19, 22]. This is part of an effort to analyze the impact of climate change on the real economy and financial sector, and to launch in earnest ESG disclosure and climate risk management [4, 12]. International research indicates that global warming has accelerated over the past decade [108, 117], with warnings that the fatal impacts of climate change are more severe than anticipated [104]. Alongside predictions of record-breaking heatwaves in 2026 [21], health security threats are also increasing, such as allergy seasons extending by up to 41 days due to climate change [8, 13]. A report also emerged stating that the European Union (EU) is significantly underestimating the debt impact of climate change, further raising international awareness [111].

sections.title: 🌱 Strengthening Climate Crisis Adaptation Policies and Exploring Agricultural Sector Responses
sections.content: The necessity of institutionalizing 'adaptation' policies to mitigate damages from climate change is being discussed in the National Assembly, with calls for the enactment of a special law for systematic responses [7, 14, 18]. The Climate Change Impact Assessment, now in its third year of implementation, is recognized as a core strategy beyond just a licensing risk [3]. The National Institute of Environmental Research hosted a forum for the agricultural sector within the Climate Crisis Adaptation Research Council, discussing inter-ministerial expert cooperation measures to address fluctuations in agricultural yields [5, 17]. In Hwasun-gun and Jindo-gun, education for stable production of high-quality rice in response to climate change is underway [1, 9, 15], demonstrating active climate adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector. Furthermore, proactive measures for agricultural water management are being pursued in the Jeonbuk region to prepare for drought during the farming season [20].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Various domestic companies and local governments are accelerating their transition to renewable energy to achieve carbon neutrality. Korea Southern Power and Hana Bank have signed a joint development agreement for offshore wind power projects, thereby accelerating the realization of carbon neutrality [24, 40, 51, 53, 61, 76, 85, 89, 91, 92, 97, 102, 103]. South Chungcheong Province and Taean-gun are also pursuing large-scale renewable energy projects, including signing a 1 trillion won offshore wind investment agreement with Singapore's Vina Group [60, 79, 83, 87, 96, 101]. Korea Water Resources Corporation is also taking active steps abroad, concretizing 1 trillion won worth of water and renewable energy cooperation with the Philippines [57, 63, 66, 68, 74, 77, 82, 84, 88]. As concerns over energy security increase due to rising oil prices [58, 64, 72, 95], the necessity for renewable energy independence, such as solar and wind power, is further highlighted [81].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Practicing Carbon-Neutral Living Through Expanded Citizen Participation and Technological Innovation
sections.content: Carbon neutrality policies encouraging citizen participation are actively being pursued across the nation. Busan City and Jeonbuk are recruiting vehicles to participate in the 'Carbon Neutrality Point System' for reducing car mileage, thereby promoting voluntary eco-friendly activities among citizens [23, 29, 36, 38, 39, 41, 44, 45, 50, 55]. Changwon Special City plans to invest 46.3 billion won by 2026 in citizen-participatory carbon neutrality support projects, including expanding electric vehicle supply and strengthening the carbon point system [26, 31, 33, 37]. Yeosu City is also emerging as a leading carbon-neutral city through the dissemination of zero-emission vehicles [25, 52]. Furthermore, Korea University has become the first domestic university to declare 'Nature Positive' with the goal of 'beyond 2045 carbon neutrality to nature restoration' [27, 32, 35], and efforts to achieve carbon neutrality through technological innovation and education are continuing, such as the introduction of Energy Storage Systems (ESS) in schools within Jeju Island [54, 90, 99]. Discussions are also underway to activate Long-Duration Energy Storage (LDES) to complement the intermittency of renewable energy [75, 100].

sections.title: 🌍 Continued International Discussion on Climate Change Science and Policy
sections.content: The international community continues to deepen its understanding of climate change science and engage in discussions to address it. CBS News is focusing on the scientific evidence supporting climate change, helping to enhance public understanding [106, 110]. International agreements for limiting greenhouse gas emissions are coming into force [113], and specific climate science-based response measures, such as methane reduction, are being explored [114]. Concerns are being raised about the impact of climate change on the preservation of world heritage sites like the Sistine Chapel [107, 116], and the importance of peatland protection is also being emphasized [105]. Furthermore, Vanuatu is expressing its stance on the UN climate change resolution, voicing its support for international climate action [119]. International cooperation, such as the business agreement between Incheon National University's Yellow Sea Research Institute and Hong Kong University of Science and Technology's research institute for climate crisis response, is also actively taking place [16].

briefing.closing: The news gathered today highlights warnings about the deepening climate crisis, alongside diverse efforts domestically and internationally to overcome it, including initiatives in finance, agriculture, energy transition, and citizen participation. This concludes today's climate briefing.
